{"query":"2026 PGA Tour Championship Series two-tier system qualification criteria","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1879,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.pgatour.com/article/news/latest/2026/06/23/pga-tour-new-competitive-structure-two-series-model-2028-schedule-changes-announcement-championship-challenger-series-track-brian-rolapp","title":"PGA TOUR establishes new competitive structure with two-series model to debut in 2028","content":"The PGA TOUR announced Tuesday it will debut a new competitive structure for the 2028 season featuring two distinct series of tournaments.
At minimum, the top 90 finishers (out of approximately 130 PGA TOUR Championship players) will be fully exempt for the following season, while those who fail to retain membership may risk relegation to the PGA TOUR Challenger Series."},{"url":"https://www.mondayq.com/story/pga-tour-unveils-two-tier-future","title":"PGA TOUR Unveils Two-Tier Future | Monday Q Info","content":"20-23 events annually, including the four major championships
The elimination of alternates is one of the more significant changes.
For 2027, the Korn Ferry Tour is expected to remain largely unchanged."},{"url":"https://www.pgatour.com/signature-events","title":"Signature Events Overview - PGA TOUR","content":"Because The Sentry will not be contested in 2026, the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am will be the season’s first Signature Event.
PGA TOUR winners from the previous calendar year, as well as the top 50 players from the 2025 FedExCup standings through the TOUR Championship (i.e., those who qualify for the BMW Championship, the second FedExCup Playoffs event), will all be eligible for the RBC Heritage.
The 2026 schedule cadence connects events through the Aon Next 10 and Aon Swing 5, two eligibility pathways that allow trending players to earn access to Signature Events and compete alongside the game’s best.
The top 10 players in the current FedExCup standings that did not finish in the top 50 in the previous season’s FedExCup standings, through the tournament leading up to the next Signature Event.
51-60 in the prior season’s FedExCup Fall standings make up the Aon Next 10 for the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am and The Genesis Invitational.
Aon Swing 5: Top five FedExCup points earners between Signature Events (not otherwise exempt)
These tournament winners are eligible for the remaining Signature Events in the current year, as well as The Sentry in the following season."},{"url":"https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/pga-tour-announces-sweeping-changes-175515540.html","title":"PGA Tour announces sweeping changes to schedule: Everything you need to know about new two-tiered system - Yahoo Sports","content":"The Championship Series will have a points structure, and the top 90 players on the points list will be retained and remain on the top-tier series.
You have a top-tier system and a second-tier system.
On the topic of clarity, it's something PGA Tour fans have not been accustomed to, ever, with the various iterations of the FedEx Cup Playoffs over the years."},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/23/tiger-woods-introduces-golf-two-tier-pga-tour-shake-up-from-2028","title":"Tiger Woods returns to introduce golf’s two-tier PGA Tour shake-up from 2028 | PGA Tour | The Guardian","content":"23-24 events spread across February to August
The ​new structure introduces true promotion and relegation, with a minimum of 90 ‌players retained on the Championship Series and 20 ‌promoted from the Challenger Series each season.
Rolapp said there were new markets under consideration for the events yet to be filled on the Championship Series, including Boston, Denver, New York, Philadelphia, San Francisco, Seattle and Washington DC.
Full eligibility criteria will be finalised by the PGA Tour later this year, including select additional exemptions for tournament winners, medical extensions and career milestones.
Rolapp will take over ‌from Jay Monahan, who ​will conclude his 10-year tenure on 31 December."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"2026 PGA Tour Championship Series two-tier system qualification criteria","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/23/tiger-woods-introduces-golf-two-tier-pga-tour-shake-up-from-2028","title":"Tiger Woods returns to introduce golf’s two-tier PGA Tour shake-up from 2028 | PGA Tour | The Guardian","content":"Tiger Woods, in his first public appearance since his DUI charge in March, was part of ‌the nine-member committee recommending the changes. The PGA Tour has announced sweeping changes to its competitive structure, approving a two-tier system with promotion and relegation to take effect in 2028. The elite-tier PGA Tour Championship Series will run from February to August and ​feature 23-24 events with $20m (£15m) purses, while the $4m (£3m) events on the Challenger Series will provide a path for players to earn their way to the top level. The ​new structure introduces true promotion and relegation, with a minimum of 90 ‌players retained on the Championship Series and 20 ‌promoted from the Challenger Series each season. The Challenger Series, which will ‌run concurrently to the Championship Series, will have a minimum of 20 events and feature emerging talent along with players fighting their way back to the top.","score":0.8138313,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nwaonline.com/news/2026/jun/24/pga-tour-approves-two-tiered-system-in-2028","title":"PGA Tour approves two-tiered system in 2028","content":"Jun 24, 2026 — The Championship Series eligibility would be determined by the top 90 players from the previous year, the top 20 players from the Challenger ...Read more","score":0.7997713,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.pgatour.com/article/news/latest/2026/06/23/pga-tour-new-competitive-structure-two-series-model-2028-schedule-changes-announcement-championship-challenger-series-track-brian-rolapp","title":"PGA TOUR establishes new competitive structure with two-series model to debut in 2028","content":"# PGA TOUR establishes new competitive structure with two-series model to debut in 2028. The PGA TOUR announced Tuesday it will debut a new competitive structure for the 2028 season featuring two distinct series of tournaments. The PGA TOUR boards have approved a series of recommendations from the Future Competition Committee, ushering in a new competitive structure that will feature two distinct series of tournaments and will debut for the 2028 season. The new model is built upon a foundation of meritocracy, with players moving between the two series – the PGA TOUR Championship Series and the PGA TOUR Challenger Series – through a formalized structure of promotion and relegation. Top players from the PGA TOUR Championship Series will also be eligible for a limited series of elevated international events, played in the fall and with the intent to be delivered in partnership with the DP World Tour as part of the Strategic Alliance between the two organizations.","score":0.5843666,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.globalgolfpost.com/featured/pga-tour-approves-two-tiered-system","title":"PGA Tour approves two-tiered system - Global Golf Post","content":"Since Brian Rolapp became CEO of the tour in June, 2025, he and the tour’s leadership have plotted the adjustments which are intended to bring the top players together more often with a schedule of 23 or 24 tournaments including the four majors, the Players Championship and an as-yet undetermined end of season structure. The alterations, presented by the future competitions committee chaired by Tiger Woods, and formally approved by the tour policy board, will establish two levels of competition, broken into the PGA Tour Championship Series and the PGA Tour Challenger Series. “We have to look forward and beyond today and over the horizon to set up the PGA Tour and our sport for the future generations of players and fans.” – Tiger Woods. “We have to look forward and beyond today and over the horizon to set up the PGA Tour and our sport for the future generations of players and fans.”.","score":0.575517,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.mondayq.com/story/pga-tour-unveils-two-tier-future","title":"PGA TOUR Unveils Two-Tier Future | Monday Q Info","content":"The Championship Series and Challenger Series are coming in 2028, but the most important details—the pathways between them—remain unresolved. As reported by multiple outlets, the PGA TOUR will move to a two-tier system beginning with the 2028 season. The Championship Series will serve as the PGA TOUR's top level. The goal is to have 120 exempt players and ensure that every player who holds Championship Series status is guaranteed a spot in every event. The season would conclude with a match-play Tour Championship featuring a yet-to-be-determined number of players at an iconic venue, potentially one that would not be capable of hosting a traditional full-field PGA TOUR event. The Challenger Series will serve as the second tier of the PGA TOUR structure. For now, the framework is clear: beginning in 2028, professional golf under the PGA TOUR umbrella is expected to operate with two primary tiers—the Championship Series and the Challenger Series—with the details of promotion, relegation and status still to be finalized.","score":0.5342973,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.73,"request_id":"0c894c2a-bf9a-4545-aa83-a4e2df59f6c6"}
